pi_items.source: Cross-referenced against multiple authoritative GPS coordinate databases (latlong.net: 37.819912, latitude.to: 37.8185). Haversine distance calculation confirms 37.8199 is 0.032 km from primary reference source.

Cross-referenced against multiple authoritative GPS coordinate databases (latlong.net: -122.478661, latitude.to: -122.4738). Haversine distance calculation confirms -122.4783 is 0.032 km from primary reference source.
Web search confirms that coordinates 37.8199, -122.4783 correspond to the Golden Gate Bridge in San Francisco, California. Verified against multiple GPS coordinate reference sources.

Calculated tolerance zone: 0.02 degrees ≈ 2.83 km maximum distance from center. This is a reasonable tolerance for a GPS navigation task in an aviation context. Cannot contradict as it is a task design parameter.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Avare open-source aviation GPS app (com.ds.avare) with FAA chart data. The Golden Gate Bridge target coordinates are verified as 37.8199° N, -122.4783° W, matching authoritative GPS databases with 0.032 km precision. The tolerance of 0.02 degrees creates a ~2.83 km acceptance zone appropriate for aviation GPS navigation. The task operates on real geographic data and real app maps, not synthetic data.
